description: LTX-2.3 22B LIP-DUB / lip re-sync via the 'LTX-2.3-22b-IC-LoRA-LipDub' IC-LoRA. You give it a talking-head video; LTX regenerates the mouth so it matches the speech on that clip's own audio track, in a two-stage HQ pass (base render + 2x spatial upscale), while preserving the speaker's identity and the scene. Use it to clean up / re-sync lip motion, or as the base for rephrasing (see _AUDIO for swapping in new speech). TIER-2 / IC-LoRA: rides a model patch + an audio-conditioned guide path, so it's its own preset (not a stackable catalog LoRA). Graph: Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo example 'LTX-2.3_ICLoRA_Lipdub_Two_Stage_Distilled'.

about.what: Re-syncs a talking person's mouth to the speech on their video, keeping their face, identity and the scene — then sharpens the result with a 2x high-detail pass.
about.inputs: One talking-head video WITH an audio track (the speech that the new mouth motion should match).
about.key_knobs: Prompt — describe the speaker and scene (helps identity/quality). Frames — clip length. The audio comes from the input video itself (see notes to dub new speech).

_TEMPLATE: READY-TO-RUN. All five weights are pinned to real, ungated HF URLs (dev checkpoint + gemma encoder + official rank-384 distilled LoRA + LTX-2.3 spatial upscaler x2 + the LipDub IC-LoRA). Run --convert-only FIRST: it skips the weight pull and confirms the converted graph (incl. the audio-VAE / AV-latent path) builds.
_AUDIO: LTX-2.3 is audio-aware. This graph reads the INPUT VIDEO's own audio (LoadVideo 5002 -> GetVideoComponents 5010 -> LTXVAudioVAEEncode 5005 -> audio ref tokens), and re-generates the mouth to match it. So a clip MUST have an audio track (a silent clip gets a silent guard track added, which gives nothing to sync to). To DUB to NEW speech (true 'rephrasing'), the audio source node would need to be swapped to a separate LoadAudio -- not wired in this single-input preset yet; tracked as a follow-up.
_HOWITRUNS: No-build path: public base image, comfy_run.py clones node_packs, downloads the weights, converts UI->API (the s1/s2 ResizeImageMaskNode DynamicCombo nodes are handled by the converter), applies patches, and renders. Two-stage + audio path makes this a heavier cold start; rtxpro6000 recommended.
_TWOSTAGE: Stage 1 renders at the EmptyLTXVLatentVideo size (3059, 960x544); the spatial upscaler x2 (4974/4975) doubles the latent and the second sampler (4971) refines it, so the saved clip is ~1920x1088. The input frames are resized to the stage sizes by the coupled ResizeImageMaskNode pair (s1 5009 = 960x544, s2 5003 = 1920x1088) -- if you change the stage-1 width/height (3059) you must edit those two resize nodes to match, so resolution is left at the graph default here.
_DISTILL: Uses Lightricks' official rank-384 distilled LoRA at the graph's tuned 0.5. If lip detail looks dampened by the distill LoRA on top of the IC-LoRA conditioning, swap to the cond-safe rank-72 variant (TenStrip ...ceil72_condsafe) by editing LoraLoaderModelOnly (5018) lora_name and this models entry. See README 'Cond-safe distill LoRA'.

ui.title: LTX-2.3 Lip-Dub — re-sync a talking head to its own audio (IC-LoRA)

primary_input.label: Talking-head video (with audio)
primary_input.tooltip: A clip of a person speaking, WITH an audio track. The mouth is regenerated to match that audio; the face/identity and scene are preserved. Frontal, well-lit faces work best. Output is upscaled 2x.

prompt.default: A person speaks directly to the camera in a softly lit room, natural skin texture and clear facial detail, calm and articulate delivery, shallow depth of field.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: Describe the SPEAKER and scene (helps the model hold identity and quality) -- not the words being said (those come from the audio). Same LTX-2.3 single-paragraph style. (src: LipDub example workflow + LTX-2.3 prompt guide)

negative.default: pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly

ui.label: Frames (length)
ui.tooltip: Frame count; LTX REQUIRES 8*n+1 (..., 121, 161). Should be >= the talking clip's frame count so the speech fits. 121 ~= 4s. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)
